overview: The XML Stream Filter processes XML documents using a stream parser, allowing it to handle much larger documents than the DOM-based [[XML Filter]]. It uses the same YAML-based configuration syntax as the HTML Filter, with all HTML Filter parameters available except `escapeCharacters`. If you need ITS (Internationalization Tag Set) support, use the XML Filter instead.

parser.assumeWellformed.help:
Controls whether the parser assumes the input XML is well-formed. When enabled, the parser uses a more lenient parsing mode that can handle documents that may not be strictly well-formed XML.

The default configurations all set this to `true`.

parser.preserveWhitespace.help:
Controls the global default for whitespace preservation. Individual elements can override this via `PRESERVE_WHITESPACE` element rules (e.g., `<pre>`, `<codeblock>`) or the `xml:space` attribute.

When `false` (the default), insignificant whitespace is normalized.

inlineCodes.enabled.help:
Activates pattern-based detection of inline codes (placeholders, markup tags, variables, etc.) within translatable text. The patterns are defined in the **rules** section below.

This uses the same code finder mechanism as the HTML Filter.

inlineCodes.rules.help:
Regex patterns that identify inline codes within translatable text segments. Each pattern should match a complete inline code (placeholder, variable reference, embedded markup, etc.).

These are Java regex patterns applied to the extracted text content.

inlineCodes.mergeAdjacent.help: When multiple inline codes appear next to each other with no translatable text between them, merge them into a single code placeholder. This simplifies the segments presented to translators.

inlineCodes.moveBoundaryCodes.help: Moves inline codes that appear at the very start or end of a segment out into the non-translatable skeleton. This keeps segment boundaries clean for translation tools.

inlineCodes.simplifierRules.help: Rules for simplifying the representation of inline codes in extracted segments. Uses the Okapi simplifier rules format to reduce complex inline code patterns into simpler placeholders.

attributes.help:
Defines global attribute extraction rules that apply across all elements. Each key is an attribute name (e.g., `xml:lang`, `id`, `xml:space`) mapped to a rule specifying how that attribute should be handled.

The default configuration handles:
- `xml:lang` — writable localizable (language tag)
- `xml:id` and `id` — used as segment identifiers
- `xml:space` — controls whitespace preservation state

editorTitle.help: Title displayed in the parameters editor UI. This is a cosmetic setting used by the Okapi GUI tools.

taggedConfig.help: Internal tagged configuration object used by the filter's YAML-based configuration system. This stores the parsed element and attribute rules from the YAML configuration file.

path.help: Path to the YAML configuration file containing the filter parameters. When set, the filter loads its configuration from this external file rather than using inline parameters.

limitations: No transparent support for namespace prefixes — element names must be declared with their full prefix in the configuration (e.g., `ns:element` not just `element`).
The filter is **not case-sensitive** for element names — `<elem>` and `<Elem>` are treated as identical, which violates the XML specification.
Does not support ITS (Internationalization Tag Set) — use the XML Filter instead for ITS support.
The `escapeCharacters` parameter from the HTML Filter is **not available** in this filter.
processingNotes: **Input encoding**: Uses the document's encoding declaration if present; otherwise defaults to UTF-8 (ignoring any externally specified default encoding).
**Output encoding (UTF-8)**: A BOM is written only if the input was UTF-8 and had a BOM. If the input was non-UTF-8, no BOM is added.
**XML declaration**: If the original document had an XML encoding declaration, it is updated on output. If it did not, one is automatically added.
**Line breaks**: Output preserves the same line-break style (CRLF/LF) as the input document.

**Quote mode**: Escaping of quotes and apostrophes can be configured via `quoteModeDefined: true` and `quoteMode` (0=unescaped, 1=all named entities, 2=double named + single numeric, 3=double quotes only).
**CDATA subfiltering**: The `global_cdata_subfilter` parameter applies a secondary filter (e.g., `okf_html`) to all CDATA section content.

**PCDATA subfiltering**: The `global_pcdata_subfilter` parameter applies a secondary filter to escaped markup in PCDATA. Only content matched by `TEXTUNIT` element rules is passed to the subfilter — `INCLUDE` rules are not subfiltered.
Uses the same YAML-based configuration syntax as the HTML Filter — all HTML Filter parameters apply except `escapeCharacters`.

examples.title: CDATA subfiltering with HTML
examples.description: Process CDATA sections containing HTML content using the HTML filter as a subfilter. This is useful for XML documents that embed HTML within CDATA blocks.

examples.title: PCDATA subfiltering for escaped HTML
examples.description: Process double-escaped HTML content in PCDATA. The element containing the escaped HTML must have a `TEXTUNIT` rule — `INCLUDE` rules will not trigger subfiltering.
examples.input:
```xml
<test>
  <p>This is embedded HTML content within a p tag.</p>
</test>
```

examples.title: Quote mode configuration
examples.description: Configure how quote and apostrophe characters are escaped in the output. Mode 3 escapes only double quotes.

examples.title: Java Properties XML with embedded HTML
examples.description: Built-in configuration (`okf_xmlstream-JavaPropertiesHTML`) for Java Properties XML files where `<entry>` elements contain HTML in CDATA sections.
examples.config:
```yaml
assumeWellformed: true
global_cdata_subfilter: okf_html
preserve_whitespace: false

elements:
  entry:
    ruleTypes: [TEXTUNIT]
    idAttributes: [key]
```

editorTitle.title: Editor Title
editorTitle.description: Title displayed in the parameters editor UI

taggedConfig.title: Tagged Configuration
taggedConfig.description: Internal parsed configuration object for element and attribute rules

path.title: Configuration File Path
path.description: Path to an external YAML configuration file

fullDoc:
# XML Stream Filter

The XML Stream Filter processes XML documents using a stream parser, allowing it to handle much larger documents than the DOM-based XML Filter. It uses the same YAML-based configuration syntax as the HTML Filter, with all HTML Filter parameters available except `escapeCharacters`. If you need ITS (Internationalization Tag Set) support, use the XML Filter instead.

## Parameters

### Parser

#### Assume Well-Formed
Controls whether the parser assumes the input XML is well-formed. When enabled, the parser uses a more lenient parsing mode. The default configurations all set this to `true`.

#### Preserve Whitespace
Controls the global default for whitespace preservation. Individual elements can override this via `PRESERVE_WHITESPACE` element rules (e.g., `<pre>`, `<codeblock>`) or the `xml:space` attribute. When `false` (the default), insignificant whitespace is normalized.

### Inline Codes

#### Enabled
Activates pattern-based detection of inline codes (placeholders, markup tags, variables, etc.) within translatable text. The patterns are defined in the rules section.

#### Rules
Regex patterns that identify inline codes within translatable text segments. Each pattern should match a complete inline code. These are Java regex patterns applied to the extracted text content.

#### Merge Adjacent
When multiple inline codes appear next to each other with no translatable text between them, merge them into a single code placeholder. This simplifies the segments presented to translators.

#### Move Boundary Codes
Moves inline codes that appear at the very start or end of a segment out into the non-translatable skeleton. This keeps segment boundaries clean for translation tools.

#### Simplifier Rules
Rules for simplifying the representation of inline codes in extracted segments.

### Attributes
Defines global attribute extraction rules that apply across all elements. Each key is an attribute name mapped to a rule specifying how that attribute should be handled.

The default configuration handles:
- `xml:lang` — writable localizable (language tag)
- `xml:id` and `id` — used as segment identifiers
- `xml:space` — controls whitespace preservation state

### Other Parameters

#### Editor Title
Title displayed in the parameters editor UI.

#### Tagged Configuration
Internal parsed configuration object for element and attribute rules.

#### Configuration File Path
Path to an external YAML configuration file.

## CDATA Subfiltering

The `global_cdata_subfilter` parameter specifies a secondary filter applied to all CDATA content. For example, to process CDATA as HTML:

```yaml
global_cdata_subfilter: okf_html
```

## PCDATA Subfiltering

The `global_pcdata_subfilter` parameter applies a secondary filter to escaped markup in PCDATA content. Only content matched by `TEXTUNIT` element rules is passed to the subfilter — `INCLUDE` rules are **not** subfiltered.

```yaml
global_pcdata_subfilter: okf_html
elements:
  test:
    ruleTypes: [TEXTUNIT]
```

## Quote Mode

Escaping of quote and apostrophe characters can be configured:

```yaml
quoteModeDefined: true
quoteMode: 3
```

- **0** — Do not escape single or double quotes
- **1** — Escape single and double quotes to named entities
- **2** — Escape double quotes to named entities, single quotes to numeric entities
- **3** — Escape double quotes only

## Limitations

- No transparent support for namespace prefixes — element names must be declared with their full prefix in the configuration.
- The filter is **not case-sensitive** for element names — `<elem>` and `<Elem>` are treated as identical, violating the XML specification.
- Does not support ITS — use the XML Filter instead.
- The `escapeCharacters` parameter from the HTML Filter is not available.

## Notes

- **Input encoding**: Uses the document's encoding declaration if present; defaults to UTF-8 otherwise.
- **Output encoding**: BOM is preserved only if the input was UTF-8 with a BOM. XML encoding declarations are updated or added automatically.
- **Line breaks**: Output preserves the input document's line-break style.

## Examples

### Java Properties XML with Embedded HTML

```yaml
assumeWellformed: true
global_cdata_subfilter: okf_html
preserve_whitespace: false

elements:
  entry:
    ruleTypes: [TEXTUNIT]
    idAttributes: [key]
```

### PCDATA Subfiltering for Escaped HTML

Input:
```xml
<test>
  <p>This is embedded HTML content.</p>
</test>
```

Config:
```yaml
global_pcdata_subfilter: okf_html
elements:
  test:
    ruleTypes: [TEXTUNIT]
```
